constraintLeaderboard.description: Critical authentication and routing infrastructure failures causing 401/404 errors across the entire application
constraintLeaderboard.goal: Reliability of LIVE features — Clock-in/out, geofencing, scheduling, payroll reports, task assignment, and Clerk auth MUST work flawlessly
evidenceFromCrawl: GET /api/auth/session → 401 (auth check failing for legitimate users)
GET /api/employees → 404 (employee management completely unavailable)
GET /api/time-entries → 404 (clock-in/out data inaccessible)
GET /api/schedules → 404 (scheduling system unreachable)
GET /api/tasks → 404 (task management broken)
GET /api/payroll/periods → 404 (payroll reports unavailable)
GET /api/activity-log → 404 (audit trail not working)
Multiple dashboard pages return empty/error states due to failed API calls
unblocks: Clock-in/out functionality becomes accessible to authenticated users
Schedule management and viewing becomes possible
Task assignment and tracking can function
Payroll report generation becomes available
Activity logging and audit trails work correctly
All dashboard and admin functionality becomes usable
constraintLeaderboard.dismissedReason: False positive — 401s from unauthenticated crawler, not real auth bugs

constraintLeaderboard.description: Critical authentication and database connection failures are blocking ALL app functionality. Clerk auth integration is misconfigured causing 401 errors across the application, and database queries are failing with connection errors.
constraintLeaderboard.goal: Reliability of LIVE features — Clock-in/out, geofencing, scheduling, payroll reports, task assignment, and Clerk auth MUST work flawlessly
evidenceFromCrawl: 401 Unauthorized errors on /api/auth/session endpoint
Multiple pages failing to load due to auth failures
Database connection timeouts in server logs
Failed API requests returning 500 errors due to DB connection issues
Users unable to access protected routes
unblocks: Users can successfully log in and access the application
Clock-in/out functionality becomes accessible
Schedule viewing and task management becomes operational
Payroll reports can be generated
All API endpoints can serve authenticated requests
Database queries can execute successfully
constraintLeaderboard.dismissedReason: False positive — 401s from unauthenticated crawler, not real auth bugs

constraintLeaderboard.description: Geofencing validation is completely broken — employees can clock in from anywhere, violating legal time-tracking compliance requirements
constraintLeaderboard.goal: Reliability of LIVE features — Clock-in/out, geofencing, scheduling, payroll reports, task assignment, and Clerk auth MUST work flawlessly
evidenceFromCrawl: POST /api/time-entries/clock-in returns 200 success even when user is 5000+ meters from store location
Geofence validation function exists but result is ignored in the route handler
No error logging when geofence check fails
Database allows time_entries to be created without location verification
Employee can clock in from home, work 8 hours at store, clock out from home — system accepts all events
unblocks: Legal compliance for time tracking
Trust in payroll accuracy
Ability to enforce on-site work requirements
Foundation for future geo-based features (shift reminders, proximity alerts)
Manager confidence in reported work hours

constraintLeaderboard.goal: Shopify Deep Sync — ensure real-time sales data streaming is bulletproof with retry logic, dead letter queues, and reconciliation checks

However: GOALS.md explicitly prioritizes 'Shopify sync stability' and 'Shopify Deep Sync completion' as IN DEV and critical
Quality target: 'Shopify sync lag: under 60 seconds from Shopify event to LIBBY data update'
Risk: Silent failures mean AI features give bad advice without anyone knowing
unblocks: AI Staffing Optimizer can trust sales data for staffing recommendations

failedAttempts.constraint: Geofencing validation is completely broken — employees can clock in from anywhere, violating legal time-tracking compliance requirements
failedAttempts.reason: Geofencing validation constraint is NOT resolved. The constraint requires that POST /api/time-entries/clock-in returns 403 when user is >100m from store, but the codebase provided shows NO geofence validation implementation in the time entries route handler. The crawl verification only checks that pages load (which they do), but does NOT verify the actual geofence enforcement behavior. The re-crawl results show WebSocket connection failures and page load issues, but these are separate from the geofencing constraint. Critical: zero of the 6 planned steps have been completed.

activeConstraint.description: Shopify webhook handlers lack proper error handling, retry logic, and validation. Webhooks can fail silently, leaving sales data out of sync with no visibility or recovery mechanism.
activeConstraint.why: This is the #1 constraint because all AI features (Morning Whisper, AI Staffing Optimizer, Midday Pulse) depend on accurate sales data. If webhooks fail silently, the entire AI intelligence layer gives bad recommendations, violating the core promise of LIBBY.
unblocks: AI Staffing Optimizer can trust sales data for staffing recommendations
Morning Whisper briefings include accurate yesterday's sales context
Midday Pulse compares actual vs goal using real-time data
Owner gains confidence that LIBBY is seeing what Shopify sees
Reduces manual reconciliation work for the boutique owner
Enables Phase 2 features (Style DNA, Win-Back Triggers) which depend on purchase history
evidenceFromCrawl: No crawl evidence yet — this is infrastructure-level
However: GOALS.md explicitly prioritizes 'Shopify sync stability' and 'Shopify Deep Sync completion' as IN DEV and critical
Quality target: 'Shopify sync lag: under 60 seconds from Shopify event to LIBBY data update'
Risk: Silent failures mean AI features give bad advice without anyone knowing
activeConstraint.goal: Shopify Deep Sync — ensure real-time sales data streaming is bulletproof with retry logic, dead letter queues, and reconciliation checks

steps.description: Add comprehensive error handling and structured logging to all webhook handlers. Wrap each handler in try/catch, log webhook ID, topic, shop domain, and timestamp. Return 200 OK even on errors (to prevent Shopify from disabling webhook) but log failures with severity levels.
successCriteria: All webhook handlers have try/catch blocks
Every webhook invocation logs: webhookId, topic, shopDomain, timestamp, processingDuration
Errors logged with context (webhook payload, error stack, severity)
Handlers return 200 status even on error (with error logged internally)

steps.description: Add webhook payload validation using Zod schemas. Define schemas for orders/create, orders/updated, products/create, products/updated. Validate incoming payloads before processing. Log validation failures with full payload for debugging.
successCriteria: Zod schemas defined for each webhook topic
Payloads validated before processing
Validation errors logged with full context
Invalid webhooks return 200 but don't mutate database

steps.description: Create webhook processing queue using in-memory queue (or Redis if available). Move webhook processing to async queue with retry logic (3 attempts with exponential backoff: 1s, 5s, 30s). Store failed webhooks in dead letter queue table for manual reconciliation.
successCriteria: Webhook handler adds job to queue immediately, returns 200
Queue processes webhooks async with retry logic
Failed webhooks (after 3 retries) written to dead_letter_queue table
Queue processing duration tracked and logged

steps.description: Create webhook_log table schema: id, webhookId (from Shopify), topic, shopDomain, receivedAt, processedAt, status (pending/success/failed), retryCount, errorMessage, payload (JSONB). Create dead_letter_queue table: id, webhookId, topic, failedAt, payload, lastError.
successCriteria: webhook_log table schema defined in Drizzle
dead_letter_queue table schema defined
Both tables have indexes on webhookId and receivedAt
Payload stored as JSONB for queryability

steps.description: Add idempotency checks using webhookId. Before processing, check if webhookId already exists in webhook_log with status=success. If yes, skip processing and return 200 (webhook already handled). Prevents duplicate processing if Shopify retries.
successCriteria: Every webhook checks webhook_log for existing webhookId before processing
Duplicate webhooks skipped with log entry
No duplicate database mutations from retried webhooks

steps.description: Create daily reconciliation job that compares LIBBY sales totals vs Shopify GraphQL totals for previous day. Log discrepancies. Run via cron at 2 AM daily. Query Shopify GraphQL for orders created yesterday, compare to LIBBY orders table, flag missing or mismatched orders.
successCriteria: Cron job defined to run daily at 2 AM
Job queries Shopify GraphQL for yesterday's orders
Compares against LIBBY orders table
Discrepancies logged with severity=warning
Job duration and result logged

steps.description: Create admin API endpoint /api/admin/shopify-sync-status that returns: last successful webhook timestamp, webhook failure rate (last 24h), dead letter queue count, reconciliation status (last run, discrepancies found). Requires Admin role.
successCriteria: Endpoint returns structured sync health data
Requires Admin or Owner role
Response includes webhook stats and reconciliation status
Endpoint responds in <200ms

steps.description: Create admin dashboard page showing: webhook health (success rate, failures, last received), dead letter queue items (table with retry button), reconciliation status (last run, discrepancies). Only visible to Owner/Admin roles.
successCriteria: Dashboard displays webhook health metrics
Dead letter queue items shown in table
Retry button triggers manual webhook reprocessing
Page requires Owner or Admin role
Data refreshes every 30s via polling

steps.description: Add webhook signature verification using Shopify HMAC. Verify X-Shopify-Hmac-SHA256 header against computed HMAC of raw request body. Reject webhooks with invalid signatures (log as security event). Prevents spoofed webhooks.
successCriteria: All webhooks verify HMAC signature before processing
Invalid signatures rejected with 401 status
Invalid signatures logged as security events
Valid signatures processed normally

steps.description: Add alerting logic: if webhook failure rate exceeds 10% in last hour, send notification to Owner. If dead letter queue exceeds 50 items, send alert. If reconciliation finds >5 discrepancies, send alert. Respects quiet hours.
successCriteria: Failure rate monitoring checks every 15 minutes
Owner notified if thresholds exceeded
Alerts respect notification quiet hours
Alert cooldown: max 1 alert per issue per 4 hours

activeConstraint.completionCriteria: Shopify webhook system meets these conditions: (1) All webhooks logged to webhook_log table with status tracking, (2) Failed webhooks retry 3x with exponential backoff, (3) Dead letter queue captures unrecoverable failures, (4) Daily reconciliation job detects discrepancies, (5) Admin dashboard shows sync health in real-time, (6) Webhook signature verification prevents spoofed events, (7) Owner alerted if sync degrades, (8) Quality target met: sync lag under 60 seconds measured from webhook receivedAt to processedAt timestamps
